pattern: House-robber variant on grouped values: aggregate spell damage by value, sort distinct values, then run a DP that skips neighbors within distance 2.
hints: Count the frequency of each spell power. If you pick value v you gain freq[v]*v damage but must skip all values in {v-2, v-1, v+1, v+2}.

Compress to sorted distinct values. For each position in the sorted list, decide: take (add freq[v]*v to the best result before any disallowed predecessor) or skip. A DP on sorted distinct values handles the varying gap between consecutive values.
Track the last two accepted values along with dp states. When the gap to the previous chosen value is > 2, it's safe to combine with the dp from that value; otherwise you must look further back.

edge_inputs.description: two adjacent values, take the larger group
values 1,2,3 — must skip 2 when taking 1 and 3, or just take 3
large values differing by exactly 2 — incompatible pair
values with gap exactly 3 — both can be taken

stress.description: random power array up to length 1000 with values up to 100
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    power = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [power]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maximumTotalDamage(self, power):
        from collections import Counter
        freq = Counter(power)
        vals = sorted(freq)
        # brute force DP with explicit state tracking
        # dp[i] = max damage considering first i distinct values
        n = len(vals)
        if n == 0:
            return 0
        dp = [0] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(n):
            v = vals[i]
            gain = freq[v] * v
            # find last j where vals[j] <= v - 3 (safe predecessor)
            lo, hi = 0, i
            while lo < hi:
                mid = (lo + hi) // 2
                if vals[mid] <= v - 3:
                    lo = mid + 1
                else:
                    hi = mid
            # lo-1 is the last safe index (0-indexed in vals)
            best_before = dp[lo]  # dp is 1-indexed so dp[lo] = dp up to index lo-1
            dp[i + 1] = max(dp[i], best_before + gain)
        return dp[n]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maximumTotalDamage(self, power):
        from collections import Counter
        freq = Counter(power)
        vals = sorted(freq)
        n = len(vals)
        # dp[i]: best damage using only distinct values among vals[0..i-1]
        dp = [0] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(n):
            v = vals[i]
            gain = freq[v] * v
            # binary search for rightmost val <= v - 3
            lo, hi = 0, i
            while lo < hi:
                mid = (lo + hi) // 2
                if vals[mid] <= v - 3:
                    lo = mid + 1
                else:
                    hi = mid
            dp[i + 1] = max(dp[i], dp[lo] + gain)
        return dp[n]
